anchorText: The sullen wind was soon awake

levels.body: The adjectives “sullen” and “awake” personify the wind.

The personification of the wind functions to mimic the speaker’s state of mind which is unsettled. The adjective “sullen” provides the idea that the wind is in a resentful mood whereas the adjective “awake” suggests rising action, thus paralleling the speaker’s disposition. The activity of the storm creates a threatening setting which foreshadows the cruel twist the poem is going to take.
The repetition of the sibilant sounds /s/ imitates the noise of actual wind. The velar plosive /k/ in the word “awake” disrupts the hissing sound of the sibilance of /s/.
The noise of wind created by the sibilant sounds stops abruptly with the aspirated velar plosive /k/ which heightens the destructive atmosphere and the impression of the finality of the poem’s plot.
